title: The `->` governing operator rendered as a neon tube sign. The tubes trace the arrow in hot pink light against the dark — only the declared path is illuminated. The glow bleeds outward the way a rule radiates authority: the transition operator defines what is permitted, and the light marks exactly that path. The neon aesthetic is the opposite of the void-arrow (43): there the arrow was carved from solid material; here it is drawn in pure light.
conceptualBridge: The neon arrow means Precept's transition operator is the only illuminated path because the definition declares the `->` as the governing symbol — in the dark field of structural absence, only the declared path glows.
decision: Kept: 4.13 avg — the neon aesthetic is visually distinctive and the pink palette is completely new; structural soundness (3 = minimum) is the key risk — neon signs don't project authority the way filled slabs do. A follow-up could make the neon form feel less decorative by simplifying the glow or using a more precise geometric composition.
